title: Which accent and empty-value symbol should identify optional semantics?
summary: Resolved in favor of a bold gold semantic question mark while retaining the std::nullopt word because ∅ rendered poorly in the owner's font.
details: Current optional markers are path-dependent: declaration overlays may color ? with the type while raw Tree-sitter conceal uses Neovim's dim Conceal color.
The proposed grammar used one accent for the ? in optional and expected type spellings and tested ∅ as a value spelling for the exact std::nullopt sentinel.

Cyan, gold, and violet accents were compared against the former production baseline across raw signatures, flipped parameters, declaration overlays, nested optionals, expected types, and value expressions.
The empty-set experiment did not rewrite source bytes or affect unrelated identifiers, but the owner rejected its appearance in the active font.
The owner selected gold for the semantic marker and explicitly retained the nullopt word on 2026-07-18; DEC-015 records the accepted production rule.

provenance.note: Owner asked for the optional question mark to be highlighted and proposed trying an empty-set symbol for std::nullopt, without choosing a color.

provenance.reference: lua/custom/dans_frontend_cpp/pointer.lua, render.lua, aliases.lua, style.lua, and highlights.lua
provenance.note: These paths own raw optional templates, structured type chunks, value expressions, and buffer-local experimental profiles.

evidence.claim: Real ext_linegrid captures compare exact spelling and RGB accents while asserting source integrity.
Synthetic matrices cover optional and expected types plus std::nullopt in declaration and return expressions.

questions: What color should the optional question mark use?
Should std::nullopt display as an empty-set symbol?
Why is the T? marker dim in raw signatures?

last_verified.method: Owner selected gold but rejected the empty-set glyph in an explicit style-lab note; the submitted catalog hash 0a4f7add82c312c0b3ffaf6e58a9904856b8c9a6ef492be01ef25ad6239627d1 and normalized resolution are preserved in style_lab/selections.json.
